title: How to build big design by stitching .il files?
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Hello, I have question about best practice to synthesise following example.
I have a big design with many sub modules. Those modules use common components from components.v verilog file. I run different synthesis for all sub modules and save them as .il files.
After this I run final synthesis where top level verilog will connect all the submodules. It works fine if I have flattened all submodules before writing .il but when modules have hierarchy then I get "redefinition" error. Would it need -ignore_redef option also for read_ilang command or something else.

## Expected behavior

I expect to be able do following.
synthesize unit A using components.v and write it to A.il
synthesize unit B using components.v and write it to B.il
design -reset
read A.il
read B.il
read top_level.v

## Actual behavior
ERROR: Parser error in line 5: ilang error: redefinition of module .....
